skill.name: diagnose
skill.description: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Use when user says "diagnose this" / "debug this", reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.
skill.summary: Use a structured feedback loop to diagnose bugs and performance regressions.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a localhost URL, markdown backtick examples, and weak-crypto patterns. Review found these are false positives: the URL is a user instruction for a local app, the backticks are markdown text, and no weak cryptographic algorithm is present. The only residual risk is a simple shell template that reads terminal input and prints captured values.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Localhost URL in User Prompt
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is part of a human instruction to open a local development app. It is not a network request or data exfiltration path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 31 passes text to a local step prompt and does not execute a network client. The URL targets localhost, so no external endpoint is contacted by the script.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Human Input Prompt
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding is a prompt asking the user whether an export action failed. It only stores user-entered text in a shell variable.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The capture helper reads terminal input and prints it later. There is no host enumeration, credential access, or filesystem inspection on this line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are markdown inline code examples and debugging instructions. They are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each location is prose in SKILL.md, not executable Ruby or shell source. The only shell file uses normal read and printf operations without eval or command substitution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm locations contain descriptive skill text and a checklist item. No cryptographic function or algorithm name is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language debugging guidance. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic implementation at those locations.

content.user_title: Diagnose Bugs with a Structured Debugging Loop
content.value_statement: Hard bugs waste time when teams jump from symptoms to guesses. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a reproducible diagnosis loop.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents through reproduce, minimise, hypothesise, instrument, fix, and regression-test phases.
Prioritises deterministic feedback loops using tests, CLI runs, HTTP scripts, browser automation, or replayed traces.
Requires ranked falsifiable hypotheses before changing code.
Supports performance regression work with baseline measurements, profiling, and bisection.
Includes a human-in-the-loop shell template for manual reproduction steps.
limitations: Requires access to code, logs, artifacts, or an environment that can reproduce the issue.
Does not include its own profiler, test runner, or static analyzer.
The shell template must be edited for each project before use.
Cannot prove a fix without a reliable feedback loop or documented manual verification.

use_cases.title: Investigate Production Bugs
use_cases.description: Turn user reports, logs, and captured artifacts into a reproducible local or staged failure.

use_cases.title: Stabilise Flaky Tests
use_cases.description: Raise the reproduction rate, isolate timing or state issues, and confirm the fixed behavior.

use_cases.title: Trace Performance Regressions
use_cases.description: Measure a baseline, compare versions, and bisect changes before applying a fix.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a Bug
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a structured debugging pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this bug using the diagnose skill. First build a reproducible feedback loop, then list ranked hypotheses before changing code.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a Flaky Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the diagnose skill on this flaky test. Increase the reproduction rate, identify falsifiable hypotheses, and propose the smallest confirming probes.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Performance Regression
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for slow code or degraded throughput.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the diagnose skill to this performance regression. Establish a baseline, compare likely versions or configurations, and avoid fixes before measurement.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Manual Reproduction Loop
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request when a human must click through the issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the human-in-the-loop template from the diagnose skill. Design the steps, capture the exact symptom, and turn the result into a repeatable verification path.

output_examples.input: The export button sometimes fails after users filter a report.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis plan with a browser reproduction loop, three ranked hypotheses, targeted probes, and a regression-test strategy.

output_examples.input: A query became slower after the latest release.
output_examples.output: A performance workflow that captures a baseline, compares versions, inspects query plans, and verifies the final timing improvement.

output_examples.input: I cannot reproduce the customer error locally.
output_examples.output: A request for specific artifacts such as logs, captured requests, screenshots, or permission to add temporary instrumentation.

best_practices: Start with the fastest deterministic loop that reproduces the user-visible symptom.
Write ranked hypotheses with predictions before adding probes or making code changes.
Remove temporary debug logs and prototypes after the fix is verified.
anti_patterns: Changing code before the failure is reproduced.
Adding broad logs that do not map to a specific hypothesis.
Accepting a shallow regression test that misses the real bug path.

faq.question: What does this skill help diagnose?
faq.answer: It helps diagnose bugs, flaky failures, broken workflows, and performance regressions in software projects.

faq.question: Does it automatically fix bugs?
faq.answer: No. It guides an agent through diagnosis, verification, and regression testing before a fix is accepted.

faq.question: Can it work without a reproducible failure?
faq.answer: It can help request artifacts and design instrumentation, but it avoids guessing without a useful feedback loop.

faq.question: Why does it ask for multiple hypotheses?
faq.answer: Multiple falsifiable hypotheses reduce anchoring and make each probe easier to evaluate.

faq.question: Is the included shell script required?
faq.answer: No. It is a template for rare cases where a human must perform manual reproduction steps.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
